summary: Backend tested for admin panel + OTP customer flow. 24/28 pytest tests passed (85.7%). Two real backend bugs surfaced: (1) /api/admin/search returns HTTP 500 when query contains regex metacharacters (e.g. '+' in phone '+3361...') because backend passes user input directly into MongoDB $regex without escaping → OperationFailure 'Regular expression is invalid'. (2) Both POST /api/auth/otp/verify (when creating any second OTP-created user) and POST /api/admin/staff/create return HTTP 500 because the email field is set to None and the startup index db.users.create_index('email', unique=True) is NOT sparse/partial, so second insertion with email:null raises DuplicateKeyError. All OTP/auth, scan, add-pizza tier logic (3/5/10 → coffee/dessert/margherita), redeem, dashboard shape, and admin-only access controls work correctly. Default admin can call /api/admin/staff/create (no 403 — the role-gate at line 434 correctly defaults to 'owner') — staff-create gate is fine; the only blocker on that endpoint is the dup-key bug.

critical.issue: Unescaped user input used in MongoDB $regex. Searching by phone '+33612345678' fails: pymongo.errors.OperationFailure 'Regular expression is invalid: quantifier does not follow a repeatable item'. The '+' is treated as a regex quantifier. Fix: re.escape(phone_clean) and re.escape(q) before using in $regex (server.py lines 422-423).

Both endpoints insert users with email=None. db.users.create_index('email', unique=True) at startup (server.py line 119) is a plain unique index, NOT sparse/partial. Second insertion of any null-email user raises pymongo.errors.DuplicateKeyError E11000 'dup key: { email: null }' → 500. Fix: replace with `await db.users.create_index('email', unique=True, sparse=True)` or use partialFilterExpression {email: {$type: 'string'}}. Existing indexes must be dropped first (db.users.drop_index('email_1')).

minor.issue: pizzas_in_period() ignores its 'start' argument and always returns the lifetime sum across all users (server.py lines 459-467). The name implies a time window. Not a hard failure but misleading and prevents real per-period analytics.
JWT_SECRET default value 'denfert-2026' is 12 bytes — below PyJWT-recommended 32 bytes. Warning logged at every encode/decode. Set a stronger JWT_SECRET in backend/.env.

integration_issues.issue: Will surface a 500 to staff whenever they search for a phone that starts with '+'. Caused by backend regex bug above.
Creating any second staff member fails with 500 because of the email null-dup-key index bug.

action_items: Fix /api/admin/search: re.escape() the user query before inserting into $regex. Without this, any phone with '+' or any name with regex metacharacters crashes the endpoint.
Fix users.email unique index: change to sparse/partial so multiple users with email=None can coexist. Migration needed: drop existing 'email_1' index in MongoDB before recreating as sparse.
Optional: implement real time-windowed aggregation in admin_dashboard.pizzas_in_period() or remove the unused 'start' arg / rename the function.
Optional: set a strong JWT_SECRET (>=32 bytes) in backend/.env to silence InsecureKeyLengthWarning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 521 lines — still under the 700 cap but adding more endpoints will push it over; consider splitting into routers (auth, loyalty, admin) before next feature batch.
admin_search builds $regex from raw input — classic ReDoS/injection vector in addition to the current crash. Escape and clamp length.
Mongo indexes on optional fields (email) must always be sparse or partial when the field can be null.
pizzas_in_period() takes a 'start' parameter it never uses — dead code path; either implement or drop.

_compute_available calculates earned via integer division on pizza_count; works but means once a user is at 30 pizzas they accumulate 10 unredeemed coffees. Worth confirming this is desired product behaviour.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_admin_flows.py (new — 28 pytest tests covering OTP, admin scan, add-pizza tiers, redeem, search, dashboard, staff creation)
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-style customers (phone +3361########, name 'TEST OTP User') and TEST staff phones via OTP and /admin/staff/create. No cleanup performed — recommend periodic prune of users whose name starts with 'TEST' if running this suite often.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_admin_flows.py -v` after the two fixes (regex escape + sparse email index). Expect 28/28 pass. If MongoDB still has the old 'email_1' unique non-sparse index, the otp_verify dup-key 500 will persist — the index must be dropped manually (db.users.drop_index('email_1')) or via a startup migration before the new sparse index is created. Frontend test was intentionally skipped (QR scanner is native-only and the UI was already statically audited).
rca_of_issue: Bug A (regex 500): server.py admin_search() at line 418-426 inserts `phone_clean` and `q` directly into {'$regex': ...}. When the customer phone is '+33612345678', the leading '+' becomes a regex quantifier without a preceding repeatable item → MongoDB throws OperationFailure 51091 → unhandled → 500. Reproduction: as admin, POST /api/admin/search {"query":"+33612345678"}. Fix: `import re; q_re = re.escape(q); phone_re = re.escape(phone_clean)` then use those in the $regex documents. Bug B (email dup-key 500): server.py startup line 119 creates a plain unique index on `email`. OTP-verified customers (line 174) and staff (line 441) both store `email: None`. MongoDB's unique index treats nulls as comparable; the second null-email document violates uniqueness → DuplicateKeyError → 500. Reproduction: create two new customers via OTP flow back-to-back, or call /api/admin/staff/create twice. Fix: change index to `await db.users.create_index('email', unique=True, sparse=True)` and drop the existing index in the running DB.
